title: acer buergerianum subsp. ningpoense
intro: Acer buergerianum subsp. ningpoense, also known as the Ningpo paperbark maple, is a subspecies of maple tree native to eastern Asia, characterized by its distinctive papery bark and stunning fall colors. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, with medicinal compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids contributing to its health benefits. The Ningpo paperbark maple has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral activities. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark of the Ningpo paperbark maple is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
